content: The orthographic and lexical origin of the variant spelling "reflexion" (the less common British English spelling of reflection) can be repurposed as a naming convention for a new LLM self-reflection mechanism that leverages inconsistent, variant perspectives on a problem to improve reasoning, aligning with the Self-Contrast finding of better reflection via inconsistent solving perspectives. This mechanism would explicitly sample multiple "reflexed" (variant-spelled, meaning variant-interpreted) versions of the LLM's own output to expose blind spots that uniform single-perspective reflection misses.
